name: RegistryEvent (Object create/delete)
definition: Sysmon Event ID 12 records RegistryEvent (Object create/delete).
trigger_scenarios: The event is emitted when the configured Sysmon operation occurs.

key_fields.explanation: TargetObject under HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run or Services is a persistence-relevant registry path; EventType CreateKey/DeleteKey identifies the operation.
Use the initiating image and ProcessGuid to join Event ID 1 command-line context.
Use the timestamp to correlate with related activity.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: TargetObject under HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run or Services is a persistence-relevant registry path; EventType CreateKey/DeleteKey identifies the operation. This is a T1112 Modify Registry signal; preserve the concrete value and correlate the initiating Image, ProcessGuid, and logon context before closing.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 12
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=12 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
